meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no project ideas (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no project ideas. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Learn how to leverage SaaS pricing models to overcome the 'no project ideas' block. Advanced case study using Antigravity and Claude Code for rapid deployment.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for the 'perfect' idea and start building high-margin pricing infrastructure. By focusing on SaaS pricing logic first, you force the discovery of business value. Use Antigravity to scaffold the engine and Claude Code to iterate on the implementation, turning abstract pricing modules into profitable, shippable client assets.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the freelancer’s graveyard. You have the technical skills to build anything, yet you are paralyzed by the 'no project ideas' syndrome. The secret isn't a better idea; it’s a better starting point. Instead of chasing feature lists, start with the most critical component of any B2B SaaS: the pricing engine.

When you build a robust, modular pricing architecture, you aren't just writing code—you are building a product that clients will pay to integrate. By shifting your focus from 'what to build' to 'how to monetize,' you unlock a repeatable framework that turns every project into a high-value asset.

steps.title: Define the Tiered Pricing Logic
steps.description: Establish a robust data schema for tiered subscriptions. This step focuses on creating a flexible structure that supports monthly, annual, and usage-based billing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a scalable database schema and TypeScript interface for a multi-tier SaaS pricing model including trial periods, annual discounts, and feature gating.

steps.title: Implement Stripe Webhook Handlers
steps.description: Security and reliability are non-negotiable. We automate the generation of webhook listeners to ensure subscription state syncs perfectly with your database.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure Stripe webhook handler for subscription.updated and invoice.payment_succeeded events, mapped to a Prisma user record.

steps.title: Build the Feature-Gating Middleware
steps.description: Create a reusable middleware that checks user subscription status before executing protected functions, ensuring your SaaS is always monetized.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a server-side middleware that checks if the current user's subscription tier has access to a specific feature flag.

steps.title: Develop the Pricing Comparison UI
steps.description: Build a high-conversion pricing component that dynamically adjusts based on the backend pricing tiers defined in Step 1.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a responsive React/Tailwind pricing table component that consumes the pricing schema and highlights the 'Pro' plan.

steps.title: Stress Test with Edge Cases
steps.description: Use Claude Code to simulate subscription upgrades, mid-cycle cancellations, and payment failures to ensure your pricing logic is bulletproof.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a suite of integration tests covering edge cases for subscription upgrades and failures in a SaaS pricing workflow.

pro_tips: Always build your pricing engine as a standalone micro-service to make it portable across future client projects.
Use 'usage-based' placeholders even for flat-fee SaaS; it makes your code significantly more valuable for future upselling.
Never hardcode pricing IDs; inject them via environment variables to allow for easy testing across staging and production.
Implement a 'grace period' logic in your webhook handlers to prevent immediate churn on failed payments.
Focus on the 'Customer Portal' link generation first; it saves you weeks of building custom billing dashboards.
content.next_steps: Package your pricing engine into a private NPM module so you can drop it into your next client project in seconds, effectively solving the 'no project ideas' cycle forever.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation to build and sell pricing infrastructure as a service. Stop guessing, start shipping, and watch your freelance revenue scale. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
